title: Method for qualitative gas analysis
abstract: A gas quality parameter chosen from calorific value (CV), Wobbe index (WI), relative density (RD), compressibility factor (Z), combustion air requirement index (CARI), methane number (MN) and others quality parameters can be directly correlated to at least one selected parameter measured in-line in a gas, corrected to preselected reference pressure and temperature and subsequently compared to reference experimental data of said selected parameter(s)collected for gas samples within a desired range of gas compositions,and at a preselected arbitrary reference pressure and temperature. A gas quality analyzing device, a computer program for determining a gas quality parameter, and a computer program product comprising instructions for performing said method.
claims:
Claims Claims 1. A method for determining a gas quality parameter chosen from calorific value (CV), Wobbe index (Wl), relative density (RD) and compressibility factor (Z), combustion air requirement index (CARI), methane number (MN), wherein the pressure and temperature of a sample of gas is measured, at least one selected parameter is measured, and the result is corrected to preselected reference pressure and temperature and subsequently compared to reference experimental data of said selected parameter(s) collected for gas samples within a desired range of gas compositions, and at the preselected arbitrary reference pressure and temperature, characterized in that said at least one selected parameter is the speed and attenuation of an ultrasound signal at one or more frequencies during sound propagation in said sample, and wherein said one or more frequency / frequencies is/are a frequency chosen from the interval 20 kHz - 2 MHz, more preferably 50 kHz - 1.5 MHz, most preferably 100 kHz - 1 MHz. 2. Method according to claim 1 , wherein the speed and attenuation of an ultrasound signal is measured using one and the same sensor or sensor set of transducer(s) according to the transition or reflection or combined measurement techniques. 3. Method according to claim 1 , wherein the attenuation of an ultrasound signal is measured using an additional sensor or a sensor set of transducer(s) working at a frequency different from the frequency at which the speed of sound is determined. 4. Method according to claim 1 , wherein attenuation is measured as the ratio of the output amplitude including actual amplificator gain levels to the amplitude recorded by the sensor. 5. Method according to any one of claims 1 - 4, wherein additionally one or more other property (properties) is(are) measured, said property (properties) chosen from thermal conductivity, concentration of CO2, N 2 or H 2 , NDIR adsorption at different spectra, and attenuation of the ultrasound signal at another frequency and/or using another transducer than used in the determination of the speed of sound. 6. Method according to any one of claims 1 - 5, wherein a statistical model/method for experimental data is created and used for calculation of a desired gas quality parameter. 7. Method according to claim 6, wherein said statistical method is adapted to specific hardware. 8. Method according to any one of claims 6 - 7, wherein said statistical model is developed using an artificial neural network or multi-parametric regression or other methods. 9. A gas quality analyzing device for determining a gas quality parameter chosen from calorific value (CV), Wobbe index (Wl), relative density (RD) and compressibility factor (Z), combustion air requirement index (CARI), methane number (MN), wherein said device comprises - a device (300) comprising an ultrasonic transmitter and receiver for measuring speed and attenuation (energy loss) of an ultrasound signal at one or more frequencies during sound propagation in a sample, - a memory (600), - a processor (700), and - optionally a pressure sensor (100), and - a temperature sensor (200), characterized in that said ultrasonic transmitter and receiver for measuring speed and attenuation (energy loss) of an ultrasound signal operate at a frequency chosen from the interval 20 kHz - 2 MHz, more preferably 50 kHz - 1.5 MHz, most preferably 100 kHz - 1 MHz, and wherein said memory stores data of speed and attenuation (energy loss) of an ultrasound signal at one or more frequencies during sound propagation in samples of gas, and gas quality parameters associated with said data, and instructions when executed by the processor causes the gas quality analyzing device to associate a gas quality parameter value to the sample and to output said value. 10. A computer program for determining a gas quality parameter chosen from calorific value (CV), Wobbe index (Wl), relative density (RD) and compressibility 

 factor (Z), combustion air requirement index (CARI), and methane number (MN), wherein said computer program comprises computer program code which, when run on a gas quality analyzer causes the gas quality analyzer to perform a method according to any one of claims 1 - 8, including the steps: - obtaining the temperature and pressure of a gas sample, - determining the speed and attenuation (energy loss) of an ultrasound signal in said sample at said temperature and pressure, - associating a gas quality parameter value to the sample based on the temperature, pressure of the sample, and the speed and attenuation (energy loss) of an ultrasound signal in said sample at said temperature and pressure; and - outputting said value. 11. A computer program product comprising a computer program according to claim 10 and a computer readable means on which the computer program is stored.
